title: Almaleea paludosa
taxonomy: Almaleea paludosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia, specifically the eastern regions. Within the genus Almaleea, it is classified as a legume. The species name Almaleea paludosa can also be referred to as a wetland pea due to its habitat preferences. Further classification of the species into a specific genus and family can be seen in various botanical resources.

intro: Almaleea paludosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as a mallee or shrub. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian bush medicine for its health benefits, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Almaleea paludosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
